audit_verdict_explanation: The independent implementation reaches identical conclusions on every counter that does not depend on the branching strategy.
Main pass returns 0 full assignments (matches checked-in 0).
Cross-check returns 184 full assignments (matches checked-in 184), of which exactly 158 are killed by vertex-circle self-edge and 26 by strict-cycle (matches checked-in 158/26).
vc_pass = 0: not a single full assignment among the 184 survives the vertex-circle filter; this is the central audit invariant and it is reproduced.

Node counts differ because the independent implementation branches rows in label order (rows 1,2,...,8) while the existing checker uses a minimum-remaining-options heuristic that selects the most-constrained center first. This is a search-order, not a soundness, difference: the set of leaves is the same, only the visited internal nodes differ.

The independent implementation therefore corroborates the existing review-pending claim that no n=9 selected-witness assignment survives the L4 + L5 + L6-crossing + indegree-cap + vertex-circle filters.
discrepancies: main_pass.nodes_visited 107018 (independent) vs 16752 (existing): branching-order difference, see audit_verdict_explanation
All four discrepancies are attributable to label-order vs MRO branching; the three counters that are independent of branching order (full_assignments, self_edge, strict_cycle) match exactly.
filters_applied: F1 pair-cap / L4 perpendicular-bisector vertex bound (each unordered witness-witness pair in <= 2 rows)
F3 crossing / L6 (shared two-witnesses cross source chord in cyclic order)
F4 indegree cap (each vertex appears in <= floor(2*(n-1)/(k-1)) = 5 rows; derived from L4 + row size)
F5 vertex-circle nested-chord strict-monotonicity (no self-edge or strict directed cycle in the strict-inequality graph after selected-distance UF quotient)
implementation_independence_notes: Did not import or re-execute erdos97.n9_vertex_circle_exhaustive or scripts/check_n9_vertex_circle_exhaustive.
Re-derived UnionFind, vertex_circle_check, witness candidates, and strict-edge generation from the geometric necessary conditions in docs/vertex-circle-order-filter.md.
Branches rows in increasing label order rather than minimum-remaining-options (intentional structural difference for cross-validation).
Strict-cycle detection uses Kahn's topological-sort algorithm (existing checker uses iterative DFS); self-edge detection by checking ro==ri before adding edge.
Cyclic-order primitives (chord_separates, angular_order_around) re-derived from cyclic_position(v, center) = (v-center-1) mod n.
Strict-edge enumeration: for the 4 angularly-sorted witnesses w0<w1<w2<w3 in row position, all 9 strict-containment pairs are emitted (matches the geometric all-containments lemma).
notes: No general proof of Erdos Problem #97 is claimed.
This is an independent audit of the review-pending n=9 vertex-circle exhaustive checker.
Soundness verdict: AGREE. The independent implementation confirms 0 full assignments survive the vertex-circle filter at n=9.
Public theorem-style use still requires further independent review of the computer-assisted enumeration.
